title: Complexity, Profiling, Rendering Budgets, and Responsiveness · Traceback Repair Bay
summary: A preserved maze trace contains one plausible coordinate, wall, graph, search, event-loop, focus, render, persistence, package, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in representative performance and responsiveness report and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive time and space from cells, passages, frontier, item count, algorithm, and representation before measuring.
Measure generation, solving, state conversion, drawing, callback delay, startup, save, and package behavior separately.
Define response, animation, cancellation, and redraw budgets and report percentiles or worst representative stalls.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Capacity Defense
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: maze-capacity-defense-1vr5j5" that uses case 3266 to separate algorithm, render, and cancellation profiles under a representative responsiveness budget. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Capacity Defense.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Capacity Defense into observable performance evidence. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Capacity Defense, keep Capacity Defense connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-capacity-defense-1vr5j5
# Competency: maze-capacity-defense.
# Case 3266: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: resource use must remain below an explicit budget.
# Changed case: inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it.
def evidence_maze_capacity_defense_1vr5j5(cells: int = 4096, solver_ms: int = 18, render_ms: int = 26, cancellation_ms: int = 41, event_budget_ms: int = 50) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1vr5j5 = "1vr5j5-5j5rv1-3266"
    assert evidence_variant_1vr5j5
    evidence_axes_1vr5j5 = (
        "grid1vr5j5",
        "topology3266",
        "search5j5rv1",
        "event1vr5j53266",
        "access32661vr5j5",
        "recovery5j5rv13266",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1vr5j5)) == 6
    if cells < 1 or min(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms, event_budget_ms) < 0:
        return False, cells, "profile-shape"
    if solver_ms > event_budget_ms:
        return False, solver_ms, "solver-budget"
    if render_ms > event_budget_ms:
        return False, render_ms, "render-budget"
    if cancellation_ms > event_budget_ms:
        return False, cancellation_ms, "cancel-budget"
    assert max(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms) <= event_budget_ms
    return True, max(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms), "responsive-capacity-profile"
